families.title: Buttons
families.summary: Primary shared button primitive for text actions and clear CTA hierarchy. Icon-only utility actions are tracked separately under Icon Button.
notes: Use `Button` for text actions and CTA hierarchy. Use the separate `Icon Button` atom for icon-only utility actions.

families.title: Inputs
families.summary: Shared text inputs and selects, plus the current embedded icon/button input pattern used in the app.

families.title: Select Family
families.summary: Shared select atom family review page covering `Select` and `RichSelect`, plus where each belongs.
notes: `Select` is for low-ambiguity choices; `RichSelect` is for choices where descriptions matter.
If visible option comparison is the point, use a decision-card molecule instead.

families.title: Tabs
families.summary: Shared content tabs. App navigation and filter-style controls are reviewed separately so the visual family stays aligned without overloading the atom.
notes: Use for in-surface content switching.
Review app-shell navigation chips and filter controls under `Navigation Controls`.

families.title: Chips & Pills
families.summary: Repeated pill and chip patterns used across onboarding, browser context, connection state, and metadata surfaces.
notes: These are currently exact app patterns but not yet a unified shared family.
`ComposerContextChip` covers removable in-field context tokens for prompt entry, including mode, folder/file, and conversation references.
`TipTapPromptEditor` (Stage 1 of `docs/plans/260429_composer_rich_chips_input.md`, flag-gated) renders the chips inline within the prompt as TipTap mention nodes.

families.title: Message Markdown Links
families.summary: Transcript and document markdown link treatment covering external links, internal file references, scope metadata, truncation, and dense prose/list contexts.
notes: This is an intentionally local markdown-renderer app pattern, not a shared Badge or Link primitive.
Internal file-reference filenames should stay in the normal markdown link family; scope/privacy labels are supporting metadata.

families.title: Navigation Controls
families.summary: Human-readable review page for navigation and filter controls that should share one visual language while keeping separate product semantics.
notes: `flow-chip` shell tabs are a separate product pattern from `ui/Tabs`, but their radius, active state, icon scale, badge treatment, and focus treatment should visually rhyme with content tabs and filter tabs.
Use this page as the comparison surface before creating or changing any segmented/tab-like local control.
Composer icon buttons live under `Icon Button` and `Hero Input`; frontmatter pills live under `Chips & Pills`.

families.title: Hero Input
families.summary: Shared prompt-entry molecule built from text entry plus inline actions, submit behavior, and optional mention/attachment affordances.
notes: This is a molecule, not a plain input atom.
Keep generic Input/Textarea atoms separate from this prompt-entry family.

families.title: Dialogs & Feedback
families.summary: Shared dialog, tooltip, and loading/feedback primitives.

families.title: Loading Indicators
families.summary: Loading taxonomy for utility spinners, mascot-spinner Rebel thinking states, and skeleton placeholders.
notes: Use `Spinner` for compact utility waits.
Use `RebelLoadingIndicator` when the product moment is Rebel visibly thinking, preparing, or generating.
`RebelLoadingIndicator` respects reduced-motion preferences by default.
Use fill-only skeletons when the destination content shape is known; skeleton shapes should not have strokes.

families.title: Registry
families.summary: Machine-readable and human-readable source-of-truth pages backing the catalog.

families.title: Toggles
families.summary: Shared toggle atom now exists, alongside a review page that documents the larger toggle realities still being unified.
notes: The atom is shared, but row-level and surface-level toggle realities still need consolidation.
Keep the Storybook page honest about unresolved larger patterns.

families.title: Icon Button
families.summary: Shared icon-only action atom, plus review context for the local realities it is starting to replace.
notes: This is now a shared atom in code, but Storybook should continue to show the local realities it is unifying.
Do not force all icon-shaped interactions into IconButton if their semantics belong to another family.

families.title: Icon Tile
families.summary: Non-interactive square category marker for cards, rows, and dashboard summaries. Icon-only actions remain `IconButton`.
notes: `IconTile` communicates category or source. It must not be used for interactive controls.
Use tone names when they carry product meaning; use semantic fallback tones for generic success/warning states.

families.title: Settings Rows
families.summary: Review page for the repeatable row structure used across Settings: labels, descriptions, badges, helper copy, and controls.
notes: Settings rows are molecules, not atoms.
The review page should focus on hierarchy and trust, not just control styling.

families.title: Headers
families.summary: Shared page and section hierarchy components for title/subtitle blocks.
notes: Use these for hierarchy, not for every card title.
Card-internal headers can remain local when they carry bespoke layout or interaction needs.

families.title: Decision Card Group
families.summary: Visible comparison group for a small number of meaningful choices, such as meeting join behavior and cloud continuity.
notes: Use for visible comparison, not low-ambiguity form choices.
Should stay calmer than marketing cards or pricing tables.

families.title: Notice
families.summary: Persistent in-flow status, attention, and prerequisite primitive for messages that should stay near the surface they affect.
notes: Use for persistent in-flow feedback; keep transient floating feedback on Toast.
Tone should be carried by icon, border, and subtle surface tint rather than heavy alert chrome.

families.title: Conversation & Suggestion Pills
families.summary: Compact recent-conversation shortcut molecule, with adjacent suggestion-chip patterns kept as app-pattern context.
notes: `ConversationPill` is not a generic badge.
Prompt suggestion chips may still deserve a separate molecule if they diverge from recent-conversation shortcuts.

families.title: Connector Chips
families.summary: Connector-chip taxonomy review page for provider, identity, status, and attention semantics in trust-sensitive Settings surfaces.
notes: This is a taxonomy review page, not a new shared chip atom.
A connector chip should answer one question clearly; if it answers provider, identity, status, and attention at once, split the composition.

families.title: Composer Suggestion Chips
families.summary: Quick-start chips near the composer input that help users start work without leaving the scan flow.
notes: These are not generic chips or tabs.
Their role is quick-start prompting near the composer.
`ConversationPill` now covers recent conversation shortcuts; suggestion chips remain an adjacent app pattern.

families.title: Dashboard Patterns
families.summary: Homepage/dashboard review surface for Home activation attention cards, recent conversation strips, stat pills, and coach carousel mechanics.
notes: This is a review surface for organisms and product patterns, not a promise that every dashboard piece is shared.
Home activation belongs in the Today attention-card family unless the product intentionally creates a larger activation surface.
`AttentionCard`, `RecentConversationStrip`, `StatPill`, and `CoachCarousel` should stay app-patterns until reuse pressure proves their contracts.
Carousel pagination and arrows belong inside `CoachCarousel` first; split them out only when another surface needs the same mechanism.
